audit_items.proof_trace: In the `fail:` block, `redisFree(c)` frees the redisContext pointer at L284; lines L287-290 then dereference `c->connection_type`, `c->tcp.host`, `c->tcp.port`, and `c->unix_sock.path` from the now-freed pointer — a direct use-after-free. No enforcement mechanism prevents this: the error-logging code was placed after the cleanup without guarding against the freed pointer.
audit_items.attack_scenario: Point redis-benchmark at an adversarial or unreachable Redis server; the resulting connection error triggers `goto fail` at L255, which frees `c` then dereferences it at L287-290, enabling heap use-after-free that can cause crash or heap corruption depending on allocator behavior.
